headline: Production voice and agent architecture dominate this cycle, even as GPT-5.6 and a federal model recall draw scrutiny.
body: The recommended channels center on building voice AI for real conversation: TEN Framework ships an open-source pipeline for streaming audio, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, and proper interruption handling via graph-based extensions [0]. AI Engineer talks reinforce the architecture theme, with a layered brand-voice approach splitting immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and a deterministic post-generation veto [5], and a three-layer raw, indexed, and wiki memory system that turns a 10,994-note vault into agent context without vector or graph databases [13]. On the model front, AICodeKing reviews GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna as competent but not revolutionary, flagging mixed benchmarks, Luna underperformance, and concerning cyber capabilities against OpenAI safety claims [1], while the Big Technology AI Summit panel centers on the federal recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models alongside infrastructure buildout and US-China competition [8] (3). Google Cloud Tech frames the agent shift directly: the model is only the brain, and the surrounding harness determines whether agents ship [10].
